description: Yarn Berry (>=2) equivalent of tasks/private-reg-scope. Bind an npm scope to a private registry and configure its auth at user level on the Renovate runner by writing to ~/.yarnrc.yml via `yarn config set -H`. Self-contained: does NOT require `base-private-reg-setup-yarn`. Can be extended multiple times, once per scope — each call targets its own registry and its own token env var, so you can chain scopes pointing to different registries. Merges into the postUpgradeTasks of Otter's built-in task presets (tasks/base, tasks/otter-ng-update, tasks/sdk-regenerate). MUST be extended BEFORE those task presets. arg0: npm scope name without the leading '@' (e.g. 'myorg'). arg1: scheme-less registry URL with trailing slash (e.g. '//repository.rnd.amadeus.net/api/npm/npm/'); used to derive the registry URL ('https:{{arg1}}'). arg2: name of the environment variable holding the Yarn-Berry auth value (base64 of 'user:password') to be used as npmAuthIdent (e.g. 'NPM_AUTH_BASE64_MYORG'); must be exposed to Renovate via customEnvVariables / allowedEnv. If your token is a bearer token rather than basic auth, replace 'npmAuthIdent' with 'npmAuthToken' in this preset. Writes to ~/.yarnrc.yml on the runner so nothing is committed. References: Yarn settings (npmScopes, npmRegistryServer, npmAuthIdent, npmAuthToken) https://yarnpkg.com/configuration/yarnrc ; `yarn config set -H` with bracket-path syntax https://yarnpkg.com/cli/config/set .
